title: Difference between color list in Status -> Index and color mapping and StatusColumnColors

posts.author: Nazariy (Nazariy)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

In API reference documentation for Status column there is given a list of columns that can be used for labels, available here:

view.monday.com

To Broadcast

On the other hand, in Managed columns documentation, colors is given as:

StatusColumnColors

An enum containing the available colors for managed status column labels

Color names are different across the list.
So, in my project i need to create/update status labels based on some condition via API - what color list should i use?And why they are differen if looks like it related to the same Status type column?

Regards,
N.

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Nazariy !

Use the StatusColumnColors enum.

The two lists are different because they belong to different parts of the API. The status column label list shows how colors are represented when you read existing labels. The managed columns API uses its own enum for writing and updating labels.

When you create or update labels through the managed columns API, it only accepts values from StatusColumnColors. The other list is not valid for those operations.

The result is that your label updates work correctly when you use StatusColumnColors, and the color is applied as expected.
